segments.s187-g49: فرودش is the proper name Farud with the enclitic -ش ('to/for him'), so here it means 'to Farud'. چنین پاسخ آورد is a fixed pattern: آورد (past 3rd sing.) 'gave/brought' with پاسخ 'an answer' and چنین 'such/thus'. باز is an adverb 'again' modifying آورد; word order here places the recipient (فرودش) and the adverb before the verb phrase.
segments.s187-g50: که introduces reported speech ('that'). تندی ندیدی is literal 'you did not see haste' — here meaning 'you have not witnessed/experienced such haste' (ندیدی is past/perfect of دیدن). تو is the emphatic subject 'you'. تندی مساز is a prohibitive imperative: مساز is negative imperative 'do not make/do not be', with تندی as its object/adverbial 'be hasty' (imperative at the end).
segments.s187-g51: سخن نرم 'gentle speech' is the object phrase placed before the verb for emphasis. گوی‌ای is the archaic split form of the imperative 'گوی ای' ('speak, O ...') addressing the vocative that follows: جهاندیده مرد 'world‑wise man.' The vocative carries the addressee; word order is object → imperative → vocative.
segments.s187-g52: میارای is an exhortative/imperative form 'adorn' (present/imperative sense) governing لب را where را marks the definite object 'the lip' (i.e. the speaker's mouth/speech). بگفتار is an adverbial/prepositional phrase 'with speech/in speech' and سرد modifies it 'cold/stern'; together they indicate how the lip should (not) be adorned. The verb is contiguous with the previous clause and continues the address.
segments.s187-g53: نه ... و ... gives a negative contrastive pair: نه تو شیر جنگی 'you are not a war‑lion' (شیر 'lion' + adjectival/epithet جنگی 'warlike/fighter') and من گور دشت 'and I [am] a gūr‑of‑the‑plain' (گور دشت a compound kenning). The pronouns follow the negated predicates for emphasis; the clause is nominal (no copula needed).
segments.s187-g54: برین گونه is an adverbial phrase 'in this manner' formed from بر + ین (an old/poetic demonstrative 'this') + گونه 'manner'. بر ما نشاید گذشت is an impersonal/formal structure: نشاید 'it is not fitting' (present/impersonal) with گذشت ('to pass' / 'that it pass') — together 'it is not fitting that this should pass upon us' (i.e. we should not endure/allow such a thing).
segments.s187-g55: فزونی نداری تو is a negative possession statement: فزونی 'superiority/advantage' + نداری (lit. 'do not have/you lack') with تو as an emphatic subject. چیزی ز من 'anything over me' uses ز as the comparative 'from/over than' — together: 'you have no excess/superiority over me in anything.'
segments.s187-g56: This segment lists the domains in which the lack of superiority holds: بگردی, مردی, نیروی تن. Here بگردی functions as a verbal‑noun/abstract 'becoming' or 'bravery/achievement' (from گشتن), مردی is 'manliness/manhood', and نیروی تن is 'physical/bodily strength'; the conjunctions و coordinate the nouns. The finite verb (نداری) from the previous clause is understood to govern these nouns (ellipsis of a repeated verb).
segments.s187-g17: ورایدونک is an archaic compound used here generically — roughly ‘if there be one’ or ‘if any (such person) exists’. باشد is a classical/existential form of 'to be' used in a subjunctive/optative sense ('there be'); ز is the poetic preposition 'from/among'; کارآگهان is a plural participial noun ‘the informed/aware ones’ (i.e. scouts or those aware of the affair). The whole segment sets up a conditional: 'if there is anyone among the informed...'.
segments.s187-g18: که introduces the subordinate clause ('that / who / namely'); بشمرد is the present/subjunctive stem 'count/reckon' and here functions as the lexical verb; خواهد is the future/auxiliary meaning 'will' that follows the main verb in classical word order (بشمرد خواهد = 'will count'); سپه را marks the definite object with را; نهان functions adverbially 'secretly/hidden'.
segments.s187-g19: همانجا is a locative adverb 'there/at that very place'. بدونیم here is used adverbially as 'without (delay)' or 'without waiting' (classical usage of بدون in an adverbial/temporal sense); باید is the modal 'must/ought to'; زدن is the verbal noun/infinitive 'to strike' — the infinitive follows باید to express obligation ('must strike').
segments.s187-g20: فروهشتن is a compound infinitive 'to bring down / to force down'; از کوه is a prepositional phrase 'from the mountain'; و connects the coordinated verb phrase; باز آمدن is another infinitive 'to come back / to return'. The two infinitives form a coordinated sequence of required actions (bring down ... and return).
segments.s187-g21: بسالار is a vocative/indirect object 'to the commander' (title), placed before the personal names for emphasis; بهرام and گودرز are proper names (Bahrām, Goudarz); گفت is the past verb 'said' (classical past), placed after the addressee and subject in this verbless-order phrase — literally 'to the commander Bahram Goudarz said.'
segments.s187-g22: که introduces the reported content ('that'); این کار 'this matter/act' is the subject; بر من is a dative-like phrase here meaning 'with regard to me / toward me' (i.e. 'from me' in the sense of concealing something from me); نشاید is a fixed classical expression 'it ought not / it is not fitting'; نهفت is the passive/adjective-like participial idea 'kept hidden' (so together 'it is not fitting that this be hidden from me').
segments.s187-g23: روم here is a first-person verbal form 'I will go' in classical usage (note: the lexeme list also admits the geographic 'Rum' but context makes it a verb); هرچ 'whatever/whatever you like'; گفتی here means 'you said' or 'you commanded' (relative clause 'whatever you said'); بجای‌آورم is an idiomatic verbal sequence 'to perform/fulfil' (بجای + آوردن, literally 'bring to the place' = carry out), with آورم 'I will bring/do' as the 1st-person present/future form.
segments.s187-g24: سر کوه 'the summit/top of the mountain' (سر = summit, followed by the noun it modifies); یکسر is an adverbial intensifier 'entirely / at once / straightaway'; بپای is a classical verbal element meaning roughly 'to set down/bring to a stand' or 'to cause to stand' in such idiomatic contexts (here part of the verbal cluster with آورم); آورم once more is 'I will bring/put'. The whole segment states the destination and completeness of the action ('I will bring [them] straight to the mountain-top').
segments.s187-g73: بدو گفت = 'he said to him' where بدو is the clitic/prepositional pronoun 'to him' (preceding the past verb), and گفت is the past tense 'said'. کز چه = an archaic interrogative 'for what reason / why' (کز + چه). ز بهرام نام literally 'of/Bahram name' = 'Bahram’s name' (ز = poetic 'of'). Word order is topical: indirect object (بدو) + verb (گفت) + interrogative phrase.
segments.s187-g74: نبردی is the past negative of the compound idiom نام بردن 'to mention' (here 'you did not mention'). و is the coordinating 'and'. بگذاشتی is past 'you left/placed/let' (you left it undone). کار خام = 'a raw/unfinished matter' (کار = affair, خام = raw/unripe). Together the clause completes the complaint introduced in the previous segment.
segments.s187-g75: ز گودرزیان ما = 'from/among the Gudarzis, we' (ز = poetic 'from/among', گودرزیان = the Gudarzis, ما = we). بدوییم is a first-person plural present/subjunctive form meaning 'we run' or 'we hasten' (classical Poetic conjugation). شاد = 'glad, joyful' modifies the clause; word order is poetic and topical.
segments.s187-g76: مرا زو نکردی بلب هیچ یاد = a compact poetic clause meaning 'you did not make any song/mention of him to me'. مرا (me) is the object (م + را split in the provided tokens), ز + و = 'of him' (ز او contracted to زو), نکردی = 'you did not do / you did not make', بلب 'nightingale/songster' is used figuratively for 'song/mention', هیچ یاد = 'no remembrance / not at all'. The order is object (مرا), oblique phrase (زو), negative verb (نکردی), then the noun phrases describing what was not done.
segments.s187-g77: بدو گفت بهرام = 'to him Bahram said' (بدو = 'to him', گفت = 'said', followed by the speaker name بهرام). کای شیرمرد = 'O brave man' — کای is a vocative particle (classical/epic), شیرمرد is a compound noun 'lion-man' = champion/brave man. This is a direct address introduced after the reporting phrase.
segments.s187-g78: چنین یاد بهرام با تو که کرد = 'such/thus the remembrance of Bahram toward you that he did.' چنین = 'thus / such', یاد بهرام = 'Bahram's remembrance/renown' (یاد used as 'reputation/remembering'), با تو = 'with you / toward you', که کرد introduces a relative/subordinate clause 'that he did' (کرد = past 'he did/made'). The clause states the content of what Bahram said, using classical word order where the relative 'که' closes the clause.
segments.s187-g79: چنین داد پاسخ مر او را فرود = 'He thus gave (داد) the answer to me (مر) from him / concerning him (او را) as follows (فرود)'. چنین داد پاسخ = 'gave this answer', مر او را = a topical/object phrase 'to me about him' (classical order), and فرود is a poetic marker meaning 'as follows' / 'in what follows' (here introducing the reported content).
segments.s187-g80: که این داستان من ز مادر شنود = the content clause introduced by که 'that'. این داستان من = 'this story of mine / this tale of mine'. ز مادر = 'from my mother' (ز = from, poetic). شنود is an archaic/persianized form meaning 'he/she heard' or 'I heard' in classical narrative (here rendered 'I heard'); in context it functions as the reporting verb 'I heard it from my mother.'
segments.s187-g9: ببیند functions as a narrative/perceptual verb ('he saw' / 'he perceived') and is followed by the complementizer که introducing the embedded clause. آن دو دلاور is the demonstrative + numeral + noun ('those two heroes'), and کیند is a 3rd-person plural verb form ('they do / they are doing') that agrees with that subject and completes the embedded clause.
segments.s187-g10: بران کوه is an adverbial phrase 'on/ upon that mountain' (بران = 'upon that' + کوه 'mountain'). سر بر ز بهر چیند is the verb phrase that follows: سر (summit/top/head) with the preposition بر ('on/upon') and ز بهر ('for the sake of / for what') plus چیند ('they place/arrange' or 'they set'). In classical order the subject (آن دو دلاور) supplies the actor for this verb phrase, so the whole means they set/raise their heads upon that mountain for some purpose.
segments.s187-g11: گر is the conditional 'if'. ایدونک is a temporal/contrastive particle ('at that time' / 'even then'). از لشکر ما is a genitive phrase 'from our army/host' and یکیست is an existential/identifying phrase 'there is one' (i.e. one such person exists). Together: 'If at that time one of our host is there...'.
segments.s187-g12: زند here is the main verb meaning 'he strikes / beats' (classical present/performative), followed by the prepositional phrase بر سرش 'upon his head' (بر + سر with the enclitic ش = 'his'). تازیانه is the object 'whip' and the following دویست (not listed in the word array) quantifies it as 'two hundred' lashes. Word order (verb before object phrase) is common in Ferdowsi's diction.
segments.s187-g13: وگر is 'and if'. ترک باشند is a conditional clause 'they are Turks' (ترک = 'Turk', باشند = 3rd-person plural present/subjunctive of 'to be'). و پرخاش جوی combines پرخاش ('assault/aggression') with جوی ('seeking/pursuing') to form a description 'and eager for attack' or 'ready to make an assault'.
segments.s187-g14: ببندد is a 3rd-person singular verb 'he binds / he will bind' (classical present/optative used as future/predictive). کشانش is a verbal/participial stem 'dragging' with the object-suffix -ش 'him/them' (so 'drag them'). بیارد is the classical form meaning 'he brings' (here 'he brings/carries'), and بروی is a motion/result element ('away' / 'to go' in context); together they list actions: bind, drag, bring away.
segments.s187-g15: وگر is 'if'. کشته آید is a classical passive/periphrastic construction: کشته (past participle 'killed') + آید ('comes') = 'is killed' / 'if he be slain'. سپارد is the verb 'he entrusts / consigns' and بخاک is the prepositional phrase 'to the earth' (i.e. buries him).
segments.s187-g16: سزد‌گر is an impersonal/agentive formation meaning 'fitting' or 'one who judges it fitting' — here used to express 'it is fitting' (an evaluative remark). ندارد is 'has not' (3rd-person), and از آن بیم و باک is the prepositional phrase 'any fear or dread about that'. Together: 'it is fitting that there be no fear or dread because of that.'
segments.s187-g33: چنین opens the reported speech adverbially ('thus'). گفت is the past verb 'said' (here used to introduce what follows). پس is a temporal/linear connector 'then'/'so'. رای‌زن is an agent noun 'the counselor/advisor'. با + فرود here introduces the addressee (or companion) — 'to/with Farud' — with با marking the relation; note that فرود is a proper name in this context, not the common noun 'descent'.
segments.s187-g34: که introduces the subordinate clause ('that' / 'that it is the case that'). این را uses the demonstrative 'this' plus the object marker را (marks the definite object). بتندی is a verbal/participial form from تندیدن/تند کردن meaning 'to hasten / act rashly' (here describing the action of rushing at someone). نباید is the impersonal modal 'must not / should not'. بسود functions adverbially/idiomatically 'to advantage / profitable' (i.e. 'it would not be to our advantage').
segments.s187-g35: بنام و نشانش is a compound of two nominal items 'name' (بنام) and 'sign/mark' (نشان) followed by the possessive suffix -ش 'his'. ندانم is the first-person present/archaic negative 'I do not know' (ندانم = نمی‌دانم). همی is an old/poetic particle adding emphasis or continuity ('indeed / at all'). Note the word order: topic ('name and mark') before the verb 'I don't know.'
segments.s187-g36: ز introduces source or affiliation 'of / from' (poetic use). گودرزیانش is the plural/collective name 'the Gudarzis' with possessive suffix -ش ('of them' / 'his belonging to the Gudarzis' depending on context). گمانم is a nominal/verb-like predication 'my guess / I suppose' (the -م marks first-person). همی again is the emphatic/continuous particle. Together this clause expresses a conjecture about origin/affiliation.
segments.s187-g37: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. خسرو is a proper name (Khosrow). ز is the poetic preposition 'from'. توران is the place name 'Turan'. بایران here names the place arrived at (a toponym); the sequence is 'when Khosrow arrived from Turan at Bayran.' رسید is the past tense verb 'arrived/reached.'
segments.s187-g38: یکی functions as an indefinite 'a certain' / 'one (man)'. مغفر here appears as a proper name or epithet (not the common noun 'forgiveness' or 'veil'), so it is treated as a name in the clause. شاه is the noun 'king'. شد is the past copular/auxiliary 'became' / 'was'. ناپدید is an adjective/verb-form 'disappeared' / 'vanished.' The clause says that a certain Maghfar/the king disappeared.
segments.s187-g39: گمانی here expresses a conjectural stance 'I suppose / perhaps'. همی is the poetic emphatic/continuous particle attached to the verb mood. آن is the demonstrative 'that' (referring back to the preceding idea). برم is a first-person singular subjunctive/optative/present form 'I will put / I may place' (from بردن in the sense 'to put/carry'); note classical Persian often uses برم for 'I put'. بر (preposition) + سرش 'on his head' — سر 'head' with the possessive suffix -ش 'his'.
segments.s187-g40: زره is 'armor'. تا here marks extent 'up to / as far as'. میان is 'middle / midst'. خسروانی is an adjective 'Khosrowian / royal' (describing the style or class of the armor). برش in this line appears as a nominal/compound element meaning something like 'upon him' or could be a textual/metrical form; none of the given dictionary senses fit perfectly in context, so it likely functions idiomatically or as a truncated compound — the whole phrase describes the armor's extent (the armor reached to mid‑torso in the Khosrowian fashion).
segments.s187-g89: فرودی is a 2nd‑person singular verbal form (classical/past sense) meaning 'you descended/you came down'; تو‌ای is a poetic vocative 'O you' (تو + ای) addressing the addressee; شهریار جوان is a noun phrase in apposition ('young sovereign/king') modifying the addressed person. Word order is emphatic: verb first, then vocative + title.
segments.s187-g90: که introduces the following clause ('that/so that' / relative link). جاوید modifies بادی to yield 'everlasting wind/breeze' (جاوید = eternal, بادی = wind/breeze); به is the preposition linking that breeze to its target, and روشن‌روان is a compound adjective/noun 'bright‑souled' (the one who is clear/cheerful) which receives the action of the breeze. The whole segment gives the effect/result/target of the preceding arrival.
segments.s187-g91: بدو گفت is the fixed classical phrase 'he said to him' (بدو = to him; گفت is the speech verb; here the dictionary senses supplied don't include the simple past sense, so treat گفت as 'said'). کآری is an emphatic/exclamatory particle meaning roughly 'indeed/yes' (classical usage); فرودم is 1st‑person past 'I descended' (فرود + ـم); درست functions adverbially 'truly/rightly' modifying فرودم. Order: speech‑marker + reported utterance.
segments.s187-g92: ازان سرو is a prepositional phrase 'from that cypress' (ازان = from that); افگنده is a past participle 'cast/fallen' qualifying شاخی ('a branch'); برست is a past 3rd‑person verb 'was broken/it snapped' or 'fell off'. The clause thus describes a branch having been cast/fallen from that cypress and then breaking.
segments.s187-g93: بدو گفت بهرام is again the speech frame 'to him Bahram said' (بدو = to him; گفت = he said; بهرام is the speaker's name). بنمای is an imperative 'show!' (بنما + ی as classical imperative form), and تن is the direct object 'body'. The imperative follows the reporting clause and addresses whoever is to perform the showing.
segments.s187-g94: برهنه modifies تن and means 'naked/bare'; نشان is 'token/mark' in genitive construction نشان سیاوش = 'Siavash's token/mark'; بمن is the indirect object 'to me'. Together the postposed phrase clarifies what is to be shown: the naked body (bearing) the token of Siavash, shown to the speaker.
segments.s187-g95: به بهرام marks the indirect object 'to Bahram'; بنمود is the past 3rd‑person verb 'he showed/revealed' (بنمود = showed); بازو is the noun 'arm' and فرود is used postpositively to describe it ('low/hanging' or 'lowered'). The sequence is standard SOV for classical poetic narration: indirect object + subject/verb + object + descriptive complement.
segments.s187-g96: ز عنبر بگل is a descriptive/ablative phrase 'with/of amber and rose' (ز = from/of; عنبر = amber/ambergris; بگل = with flowers/rose‑scent). بر یکی خال بود is a copular clause 'upon one mole there was' (بر = on; یکی خال = one mole; بود = was). Together they report that on that arm (or body) there was a single mole associated with the scent/colouring of amber and rose.
segments.s187-g153: و دیگر که با ما دلش نیست راست is a clause introduced by و 'and' + دیگر 'another/one more'. که introduces a relative/content clause describing that person. با ما دلِش نیست is literally 'his heart is not with us' (دل + possessive ش + نیست), a fixed idiom meaning 'he does not favor/side with us'. راست functions as an emphatic particle 'indeed/true' confirming the statement that follows.
segments.s187-g154: که introduces reported content: 'that he wanted kingship with Fariburz.' شاهی here is a noun 'kingship/throne' (object of خواست). همی is an emphatic/classical adverb meaning 'indeed/just' (here reinforcing the statement). خواست is past tense 'he wanted/sought', so the clause is a past report about that person's desire.
segments.s187-g155: مرا گفت بنگر combines مرا ('to me', object marked by را) + گفت (past 'said') which introduces quoted or reported speech; بنگر is an imperative 'look'/'see' that begins the reported command. Word order is typical: indirect object + verb + reported imperative.
segments.s187-g156: چو رفتی is a temporal subordinate clause 'when you go/when you have gone' (چو = when; رفتی = 2nd-person past/perfect). مپرسش is a negative imperative 'do not ask him' (مـ- negative prefix + پرسش from پرسیدن). که از بهر چیست is an indirect question 'what/for what reason is he (there)' with تاجی (چی) and copula ست, so the whole sentence forbids asking the man's purpose.
segments.s187-g157: بگرز is an imperative 'turn back/return'. و links actions. بخنجر is instrumental 'with a dagger' (a terse poetic form), and سخن گوی here means 'speak (with) words' or more idiomatically 'let the dagger speak' — گوی derived from گفت/گو used nominally/verbally. بس 'enough' closes the command: do that and be done.
segments.s187-g158: چرا باشد این روز بر کوه‌کس is a rhetorical question. چرا 'why'; باشد is a subjunctive/optative copula here meaning 'should be' or simply 'be'; این روز 'this day'; بر + کوه‌کس 'upon the mountain-dweller' i.e. 'belonging to a mountain-dweller.' The phrase asks why the day should favor that mountain-dweller.
segments.s187-g159: بمژده من آیم begins with بمژده 'with good tidings' (or 'bringing good news') followed by من آیم 'I will come / I come' (آیم is the present/subjunctive/classical form for 'I come'). چنو گشت رام is a temporal clause 'when/once he became thus tame' (چنو = in this way/thus; گشت = became; رام = tame). The clause groups the arrival with the condition of the other's being tamed.
segments.s187-g160: ترا پیش لشکر برم shadkam is a promise: ترا (to you; ت + را) is the 2nd-person object, پیش لشکر 'before the army' is the goal/location, and برم is a 1st-person present/subjunctive 'I will bring/carry' (classical Persian use). شادکام 'joyful/fortunate' describes the speaker's intended emotional/result state after accomplishing this.
segments.s187-g81: مرا گفت is literally 'to me he said' (object phrase before verb is normal in classical Persian). چُـون introduces a temporal subordinate clause 'when'; پیشت 'before you / in your presence' follows the particle and the verb آید 'comes' is in the present-form used for a simple temporal/future event. سپاه (army/host) appears after the verb as the (topicalized) subject of آید.
segments.s187-g82: پذیره شو is an imperative construction: پذیره (reception/acceptance) + شو (imperative of شدن 'become') = 'be receptive / receive'. و connects two commands. نام بهرام خواه places the object (نام 'the name') with its modifier (بهرام) before the verb خواه, here an imperative/command meaning 'seek/wish for' — so 'seek the name Bahram.'
segments.s187-g83: دگر is a premodifier meaning 'another' or 'a different', applying to نامداری 'renown/reputation'. ز is the archaic/poetic preposition 'from' introducing the source phrase کنداوران (literally 'those who dig/excavate'), so the phrase reads 'another renown from the excavators.' Word order is noun phrase + poetic 'ز' + source.
segments.s187-g84: کجا is an interrogative/exclamatory 'where'. Typical Persian possessive order is noun + pronoun, so نام او means 'his name.' زنگه شاوران is a poetic compound where زنگه (here 'peal / ringing') combines with شاوران ('counsellors, advisors'); the whole nominal phrase is inverted for poetic effect: 'Where is his name among the counsellors' ringing?'
segments.s187-g85: همانند is a preposition 'like' taking the following noun phrase. همشیرگان ('milk-siblings') is a fixed classical term (siblings who were nursed by the same woman). پدر follows to mark the relation 'like milk-siblings of (/to) the father' — a compact, elliptical simile common in epic diction.
segments.s187-g86: سزدگر is an agentive/participial formation meaning roughly 'if it be fitting' or 'one who deems it proper' and introduces a conditional/advisory sense. بر ایشان بجویی puts بر as the preposition 'against/toward' with ایشان 'them' as object and بجویی (present/subjunctive form with prefixed ب-) 'you seek/pursue'. گذر here functions as 'pass (by)/move on' in the older epic usage; the clause as a whole says 'if it is fitting, seek them and pass on.'
segments.s187-g87: بدو گفت is 'to him he said' (بدو 'to him/unto it' + گفت 'he said'), a common verb phrase order in classical Persian. بهرام is the speaker/name. کای is a vocative particle 'O' (also an archaic royal/title form) and نیکبخت is an epithet 'fortunate, well‑fated' addressing the addressee.
segments.s187-g88: تویی is the emphatic copula 'thou art' (تویی = تو + ī). بار here is used metaphorically as 'burden/responsibility' or 'support' — in context 'pillar/support' of something. آن خسروانی درخت is a genitive/attributive noun phrase 'that royal tree' (خسروانی modifying درخت), so the predicate states 'you are the support/burden of that royal tree.'
segments.s187-g41: 'ز' is the poetic preposition 'from' marking origin; the phrase literally reads 'from Goudarz has verily lineage'. 'دارد' is present indicative 'has' and 'همانا' is an emphatic adverb ('verily'), while 'نژاد' is the noun 'lineage/stock' that 'دارد' governs.
segments.s187-g42: The clause is an impersonal/indefinite construction: 'یکی' = 'someone'; 'لب' literally 'lip' used as 'mouth/speech'; 'بپرسش' (بـ + پرس + ش) is a verbal form meaning 'to ask him' (infinitive/subjunctive sense); 'بباید' is the modal 'must/ought'; and 'گشاد' is an adjective 'open' used predicatively — together: 'someone must open a mouth to ask him'.
segments.s187-g43: 'چو' is the temporal conjunction 'when'; 'بهرام' is the subject; the verb phrase 'بر شد' (= بر + شد) means 'mounted up' or 'rose onto' (بر as on/upon + شد 'mounted/happened'); 'ببالای تیغ' is the locative 'to the top/point of the sword' (ببالای = 'up/on high').
segments.s187-g44: 'بغرید' is a past/perfective verb 'it roared/bellowed'; 'برسان' here functions adverbially/verbally as a kind of resultive/secondary verb ('bringing/causing/reaching') and links to the following noun phrase; 'غرنده میغ' is the adjective + noun 'the roaring blade' (غرنده = roaring, میغ = blade), so the sequence yields 'the roaring blade bellowed' with a vivid, compressed poetic ordering.
segments.s187-g45: 'چه مردی' functions as an indefinite 'a certain man' (literally 'what man'); 'بدو' = 'to him' (indirect object); 'گفت' is the past tense 'said' (here used as reported speech introducer); and 'بر کوهسار' is the locative phrase 'on the mountainside'.
segments.s187-g46: 'نبینی' is second-person present negative/subjunctive 'do you not see'; 'همی' is an adverbial particle adding durative/emphatic force ('even now/still' or 'indeed'); and 'لشکر بیشمار' is the direct object phrase 'the countless army/host' (لشکر = army, بیشمار = innumerable).
segments.s187-g47: 'همی' again is an emphatic/durative particle ('do you still/indeed'); 'نشنوی' is the second-person present negative 'you do not hear' (ن + شنوی); and 'ناله بوق و کوس' is a genitive/construct phrase 'the lament/cry of horn and drum' (ناله = wail/cry, بوق = horn, کوس = war-drum).
segments.s187-g48: 'نترسی' is second-person negative 'you do not fear / are you not afraid'; 'ز' here marks cause or object of fear 'of/for'; 'سالار بیدار طوس' is a titled name cluster: 'سالار' = 'commander/chief', 'بیدار' = 'awake/alert' (adjective), and 'طوس' is the proper name Tūs — together 'the alert commander Tūs'.
segments.s187-g105: ببهرام گفت is a short speech-mark clause: the prefixed preposition بِ (here written attached) + بهرام yields 'to Bahram' (i.e. 'he said to Bahram'), and گفت is the past tense 3rd-person singular 'said'. ای is the vocative particle 'O!' and سرافراز مرد are two words in direct address: سرافراز an epithet 'exalted/proud' modifying مرد 'man/hero'.
segments.s187-g106: A string of epithets joined by و: جهاندار is an honorific ('lord/ruler of the world'), بیدار is an adjective 'awake/alert', and شیر نبرد is a compound epithet where شیر 'lion' is qualified by the noun نبرد 'battle' (i.e. 'lion of battle'). The coordinating و simply links the epithets in apposition to مرد.
segments.s187-g107: دو چشم من is literally 'my two eyes' and here functions as an emphatic phrase (like an oath or vivid expression). ار is the archaic conditional 'if'; زنده is the adjective 'alive'; دیدی is 2nd-person past 'you saw' (the protasis of the conditional); پدر at the end is a vocative 'father' addressing the listener.
segments.s187-g108: همانا is an emphatic particle 'truly/indeed'. نگشتی is the past form of گشتن used here in a counterfactual/result clause sense ('you would have become' / 'you would turn'), taking the conditional context from ار. از ... شادتر is a comparative construction: ازین (از + این 'than this') marks the standard of comparison and شادتر is the comparative adjective 'happier'.
segments.s187-g109: که introduces a content/relative clause 'that' or 'for'. دیدم is 1st-person past 'I saw'; ترا is the object 'you' expressed by the 2nd-person enclitic ت plus the object marker را (here written separately in the word list). شاد و روشن‌روان are predicative adjectives describing 'you' ('happy and bright‑soul(ed)').
segments.s187-g110: A series of appositive epithets linked by و: هنرمند 'skillful/artful', بینادل is a rare/uncertain word (here preserved as an epithet without a clear gloss), and پهلوان 'champion/hero'. The adjectives are coordinating attributes of the addressed person.
segments.s187-g111: This phrase contains archaic/prepositional material split in the word list: ب + دان corresponds to classical بدان 'to that' (بِ + آن), and آمدستم is the 1st-person past archaic 'I came / I approached'. Likewise ب + دین corresponds to بدین 'to this' (بِ + این) and تیغ‌کوه is a place-name ('Tigh‑kuh' or 'blade‑mountain'). The whole segment reads 'I came to that place, to this Tigh‑kuh.'
segments.s187-g112: که introduces a relative/explanatory clause 'who/for', از is the preposition 'of/from/among', نامداران is the plural noun 'the renowned/illustrious ones', ایران specifies 'of Iran', and گروه is 'group/band' (here meaning a company or band of men). The word order is a descriptive nominal phrase: 'a group from the famed men of Iran.'
segments.s187-g169: بدو is an indirect-object clitic-like phrase meaning 'to him/unto him' placed before the verb; داد is the main verb 'gave'; و گفت links a following speech act ('and said') — note گفت is here the verb 'said' (the provided senses list does not include the verbal sense). این ز من یادگار is the noun phrase quoted: این = 'this' (demonstrative), ز (poetic preposition = از) = 'from', من = 'me', یادگار = 'memento/remembrance'. Word order is typical older Persian: indirect object + verb, then conjunction + verb, then the quoted noun phrase as the thing given.
segments.s187-g170: همی is an adverbial particle adding continued/habitual force ('still/always' or emphatic 'do keep'); دار here functions as the verb 'hold/keep' (classical form), and تا introduces a temporal subordinate clause 'until'. خودکی is a compound adverbial ('خود' + interrogative/relativizer 'کی') used idiomatically as 'when/if it itself' or 'ever when'; آید is the present/subjunctive third-person singular 'comes', and بکار is a verbal phrase 'to come to use'/'be put to use' — together: 'keep it continually until it ever comes to use.'
segments.s187-g171: چو is a subordinating conjunction meaning 'when' (introducing a temporal clause). طوس سپهبد is the noun + title ('Tūs, the spahbed'); پذیرد is the third‑person singular present 'accepts' or 'takes'; خرام here denotes manner ('with a striding/proud gait' or 'to stride/prance') and functions as a manner complement describing how they will behave when the condition is met. The clause therefore means 'When Tūs the spahbed accepts (it), [then] (we) with proud stride...'.
segments.s187-g172: بباشیم is a first‑person‑plural optative/subjunctive/jussive form meaning 'let us be' / 'we shall be' (classical Persian use to state how the speakers will be); روشن‌دل and شادکام are predicative adjectives linked by و ('and') describing the state ('clear‑hearted/bright‑souled and fortunate/happy'). The verb precedes the predicates in this stylistic, older Persian order.
segments.s187-g173: جزین ('جز' + demonstrative 'این') here functions as 'besides/except these' introducing an additional list of items; هدیه‌ها is 'gifts/offerings' (plural). باشد is a classical copular/existential form here meaning 'there was/were' (used to introduce the existence/possession of the following items). The conjunction و links the enumerated items اسپ (horse) and زین (saddle).
segments.s187-g174: This continues the list: بزر is an attributive adjective 'great' modifying افسر (here 'helmet' rather than 'officer' — an archaic poetic usage), و links to the next noun phrase خسروانی نگین, where خسروانی is an adjective 'royal/Khosrowian' modifying نگین 'gem' or 'jewel'. The phrase therefore lists 'great helmets and royal gems.'
segments.s187-g25: بزد is a classical past 3rd-person singular of زدن ('he struck'); it takes اسپ (steed) as its direct object. The coordinating و links that verb to the following راند, so the clause reads 'he struck the steed and drove (it)'. راند is also past 3rd-person (راندن, 'drove') and از میان گروه is a prepositional phrase 'through/out of the middle of the group/troop' (از = 'from/out of', میان = 'middle/among', گروه = 'troop/company').
segments.s187-g26: پراندیشه is an adjectival/participial description 'full of thought, pensive' modifying the subject; بنهاد is the past of نهادن ('he put/placed/set') used here in a concrete sense 'he set/turned (his) head'. سر (here physical 'head') is the object of بنهاد and سوی کوه is the directional phrase 'toward the mountain' (سوی = 'toward', کوه = 'mountain'). Word order is adjective phrase + verb + object + direction, typical of poetic/classical Persian compression.
segments.s187-g27: چنین here is an adverb 'thus/so' and precedes گفت (classical 'he said' — note the provided lemma senses list is nonstandard but the function is the speech-verb). پس functions as a connective 'then' or 'thereupon'. نامور is the speaker's name or epithet 'Namavar' (lit. 'renowned') and با تخوار means 'together with the wretch' (با = 'with', تخوار = derogatory 'wretch/lowly one'). The whole segment sets up reported speech.
segments.s187-g28: که introduces the indirect question/relative clause 'who/which' (که = 'that/who'). این کیست کامد is an older-order phrasing: این = 'this', کی = 'who', ست = copula 'is' and کامد (classical form of آمدن) = 'comes/arrives' — together 'who is this who comes'. چنین خوارخوار is a predicative phrase 'so contemptible/so wretched', where خوارخوار is a reduplicated adjective used contemptuously. Word order follows classical Persian: relative + demonstrative + copula + verb + descriptive phrase.
segments.s187-g29: همانانیندیشد is a single classical-form verb meaning 'he thinks likewise / he thinks the same'. از here functions as 'about/of' (introducing the object of the thought), ما = 'us', and همی is a classical adverb (here emphatic, 'indeed/also'). The clause therefore means 'he thinks the same of us'.
segments.s187-g30: بتندی is a present/future-oriented classical form from تندیدن ('to hurry/become swift' or 'to rage'), here 'will hasten' or 'hasten forth'. برآید is the subjunctive/presentive classical form of برآمدن 'to rise/ascend' ('will rise/come up'). ببالا is a directional adverb 'upward/on high' and a final همی again adds an emphatic/continuous sense. Together: 'he will swiftly rise up.'
segments.s187-g31: یکی باره‌ای is an idiomatic classical timing phrase 'in one instant / all at once' (یکی = 'one / a certain', باره‌ای = 'occasion/instance' used adverbially). برنشسته is a participial form 'having mounted / seated upon' (literally 'seated on'), and سمند is 'steed/warhorse' — together 'a man, mounted on a steed' or 'seated on his steed'.
segments.s187-g32: بفتراک is an older/poetic adverbial or compound prepositional element (here functioning to mark manner/position — 'suddenly' or 'in separation' in the printed senses), and بربسته (from بستن) means 'fastened/tied' or 'bound'. دارد is the present/classical auxiliary 'has/holds' and کمند is 'lasso/snare'. The phrase describes that he 'had the snare bound (held) tightly' while mounted.
segments.s187-g145: همی is a poetic imperfective/adverbial particle meaning 'continually' or 'keeps (doing)'. گوید is the older present/epistolary form 'he says' (3rd singular). از marks the origin/possession here ('from/of'). تخمه نوذرم is a genitive phrase 'the seed/offspring of Nowzar' (poetic style often omits an explicit ezāfe); تخمه = 'offspring' and نوذرم refers to the king Nowzar.
segments.s187-g146: جهان را uses را as the definite object marker ('the world' as the thing affected). بشاهی is a noun/adverbial 'to/into kingship' (بـِ + شاهی = 'into kingship' or 'rule'); خود here emphasizes possession ('my/own'). اندر is a locative/prepositional adverb 'into/in', and خورم is the 1st-person present/intentional form of خوردن used idiomatically ('I will take/receive/possess' — in epic Persian it often means 'to take/bring under one’s control').
segments.s187-g147: سزدگر is an agentive/nominal phrase 'one who considers it fitting' (سزد = 'it is fitting', -گر agentive). بپیچد is the old present/subjunctive form (ب‑ prefix + پیچد) meaning 'twists/turns (words)' — here a conditional/hypothetical 'if he twists'. ز is the poetic preposition 'from/because of', گفتار 'speech/words', and من is the possessor 'my'.
segments.s187-g148: گراید is a verbal form meaning 'he turns/tends' (present indicative/subjunctive nuance). بتندی (ب‑ + تندی) means 'becomes swift/angry/fierce' — here 'takes offense / becomes enraged'. ز again = 'because of / from', کردار = 'deed, action', and من = 'my'. The pair shows consequence: if he twists my words, he will (in turn) be angered by my actions.
segments.s187-g149: جز از من is a prepositional phrase 'except for me'. هرآنکس که آید is a relative clause 'whoever comes' (که introduces the clause; آید is present/optative 'comes'). برت is a prepositional phrase 'upon you / to you' (برت = بر + تو with contraction), so the whole segment sets up 'Except me, whoever comes to you...'.
segments.s187-g150: نباید is an impersonal modal 'it must not / should not (be allowed)'. که introduces the subordinate clause. بیند is the 3rd‑person present subjunctive of دیدن 'to see/receive' (here 'to obtain' or 'to be given'). سر و مغفرت is a coordinated object: سر (here idiomatically 'honor/head/privilege') and مغفرت 'forgiveness/pardon'. The clause says that such a person should not obtain honor and pardon.
segments.s187-g151: که introduces an explanatory clause 'for/that'. خودکامه is an adjective/noun 'self‑willed, autocratic'. مردیست is an archaic fused form of مردِ است ('he is a man' or 'there is a man'); the -ی‑st ending is an older poetic copula. بی + تار و پود = 'without warp and weft' (a poetic idiom meaning lacking proper fabric/structure or fundamentals).
segments.s187-g152: کسی دیگر آید is 'if/when any other person comes' (کسی دیگر = 'someone else/any other', آید = 'comes'). نیارد درود is an archaic negative/result clause: نیارد (poetic/older form related to 'نیاورد') means 'will not bring/produce', and درود = 'greeting/salutation'; together 'no other who comes will bring greeting' (i.e. others will not greet or show homage).
segments.s187-g1: چو is a temporal conjunction 'when'. ایرانیان is the subject 'the Iranians'. از بر + کوهسار is a prepositional phrase meaning 'from the side of the highland / hillside' (از بر is a poetic compound preposition: از = 'from' and بر = 'on/upon' combining to indicate origin or vantage on the slope). Word order: conjunction → subject → prepositional phrase.
segments.s187-g2: بدیدند is past 3rd-person-plural 'they saw'. جای governs the following nouns as 'a place/spot'. فرود is a noun 'descent; place to alight' and is coordinated with تخوار by و. تخوار here functions as a second substantive describing some sort of camping/low-place tied to the notion of descent; the exact lexical sense is archaic/uncertain in the given senses (see senses mapping). The whole noun phrase 'جای فرود و تخوار' is the object of بدیدند.
segments.s187-g3: برآشفت is a past-tense verbal phrase 'became angry/was enraged'. ازیشان (از + ایشان) expresses the cause/target 'because of them / at them'. سپهدار طوس is a title + name (سپهدار 'the commander' + طوس 'Tūs') and functions as the grammatical subject that follows the participial/verb phrase in this poetic word order.
segments.s187-g4: فروداشت is a transitive past verb 'he brought down / caused to descend / he set (something) down' with سپهدار طوس as its agent. بر جای پیلان و کوس is a prepositional phrase 'upon/into the place of the elephants and the drums' (پیلان = elephants in plural; کوس = war-drums). The verb controls this phrase as the target/location of the action.
segments.s187-g5: چنین is an adverb 'thus / in this way'. گفت here is the reporting verb 'he said' (the provided sense list does not include the verbal sense, see senses mapping). کز (که + ز contracted) introduces a partitive/relational phrase 'from / of which' and links to لشکر نامدار 'the famed army/host' as the source referenced by what is said.
segments.s187-g6: سواری is 'a rider / horseman'. بباید is a modal/present form meaning 'is needed / ought to be' (here impersonal/necessitative: 'a rider is required'). کنون is the adverb 'now'. نیک‌یار is either a vocative/name ('Nīk-yār') or an epithet 'good-friend'; in syntax it follows the clause as the addressee or apposition. Word order is noun + modal + time + vocative/name.
segments.s187-g7: که here introduces a result/purpose clause ('so that'). جوشان شود is a stative/result expression: جوشان (literally 'boiling, seething') + شود ('become' / subjunctive/optative) = 'may surge/become agitated'. زین میان means 'from among these saddles' (زین = 'saddle'; میان = 'among/middle'), and گروه is the subject 'a band / troop' that will surge forth. The clause expresses purpose/result: 'so that a troop from among the saddles may surge.'
segments.s187-g8: This phrase describes the extent/direction of the movement: برد + اسپ conveys the action of taking/bringing horses (برد here is verbally used but the provided senses do not include the verbal sense; see mapping). تا indicates extent 'up to / as far as'. بر جای/بر سر تیغ کوه = 'upon the summit (بر سر) of the mountain's blade (تیغ used metaphorically for a sharp ridge)'. The whole string says the troop rushes with/onto horses as far as the razor-like mountain top.
segments.s187-g57: A polysyndetic list of bodily and mental faculties joined by و (and). Each noun is presented in simple citation form; Persian does not mark case here, so the whole string functions as a coordinated noun phrase (no verb in this segment).
segments.s187-g58: زبانی سراینده is a compact genitive/attributive phrase meaning 'the poet's tongue' or 'the poet's speech' (in poetry the ezāfe can be omitted or contracted). چشم و گوش are then added as further items (eye and ear).
segments.s187-g59: نگه کن بمن is an imperative: نگه (gaze/look) + کن (do) = 'look!' with the indirect object بمن ('at me'). تا introduces an explanatory clause 'for / insofar as' (here: 'for I too have them'), followed by مرا (me) + را (object marker) and نیز 'also', finished by the copula هست 'is/exists' — together meaning 'for I also have (them)'.
segments.s187-g60: اگر هست is a conditional 'if it is so' (اگر = if; هست = 'is/exists'). بیهوده is an adverb 'in vain', and منمای is a negative imperative 'do not show/do not display' (an archaic/poetic form meaning 'do not move/reveal'), with دست ('hand') as its object: 'do not show your hand in vain.'
segments.s187-g61: This line uses a compact classical shape: سخن (a word/question) followed by پرسمت‌گر, here a fused conditional form meaning 'if I ask you' (پرسم = 'I ask', ت = clitic 'you', گر = 'if'). تو پاسخ‌دهی is 'will you answer?' with تو (you) and پاسخ‌دهی (giving an answer / replying).
segments.s187-g62: شوم شاد is an optative/clause 'I would become glad' (شوم = 'I may become' / 'I would be', شاد = 'happy'). اگر introduces the condition 'if', رای فرخ = 'a fortunate plan/counsel' (رای here = plan/counsel), and نهی is an archaic 2nd‑person form meaning 'you put/offer/bring forward' (i.e. 'if you offer [such counsel]').
segments.s187-g63: بدو گفت بهرام is the standard classical word order 'to him said Bahram' (بدو = 'to him', گفت = 'said', بهرام = subject 'Bahram'). بر گوی is a prepositional phrase 'upon the sphere/globe' (بر = on/upon, گوی = orb), and هین is an interjection/command 'hither/ho/stop' used to call attention or order action.
segments.s187-g64: A parallel locative statement: تو بر آسمانی 'you are on/in the heavens' and من بر زمین 'and I (am) on the earth'. Persian often omits the copula in such predicative locative phrases; بر + noun marks position.
segments.s187-g137: بگویم is the 1st‑person present/subjunctive form (classical usage often renders future or 'I will') of گفتن, placed before the subject من (verb–subject order). این هرچ گفتی is a noun phrase 'this / all that you said' where گفتی is the past 2nd‑person 'you said' functioning as a relative clause modifier of هرچ. بطوس is the proper name (the addressee of what will be told).
segments.s187-g138: بخواهش is built from بخواه (to ask/seek) plus the object clitic -ش 'him', so together with the following دهم it forms the periphrasis 'I will ask him' (دهم as the 1st‑person present/subjunctive of دادن used here as the auxiliary/causative). نیز is an adverb 'also', and بر + دست‌بوس indicates relation/role ('as/to a hand‑kisser'); دست‌بوس is a noun 'hand‑kisser' or sycophant.
segments.s187-g139: ولیکن is a conjunction 'but/yet' introducing contrast. سپهبد is the title 'spahbed' (a military commander) and خردمند is a predicate adjective 'wise'; the sentence ends with the copula/negator نیست 'is not' (standard nominal sentence: subject + predicate + نیست).
segments.s187-g140: سر و مغز او is a coordinate phrase 'his head and brain' (سر = head, مغز = brain). از در پند نیست is a negative existential/possessive idiom: از + noun phrase 'from/of counsel/advice' (در پند = 'in counsel') plus نیست 'is not', meaning roughly 'he lacks counsel' or 'there is no advice in his head'.
segments.s187-g141: هنر دارد is noun + present 'has skill' (هنر = skill/art, دارد = has). و links the next noun خواسته ('desire' / 'wish'), and هم‌نژاد here follows as an adjective/phrase meaning 'of the same kind/race'—together the phrase says he has skill and desire but the next clause will contrast his lineage or like quality.
segments.s187-g142: نیارد is a classical negative 3rd‑person singular (here corresponding to نیاورد / 'does not bring' or 'does not possess/hold'); همی is an emphatic adverb 'still/indeed'. بر دل is the locative 'onto the heart' (بر + دل), از شاه یاد = 'memory of the king' (از = 'of/about', شاه = king, یاد = memory). Together: 'he does not put the king's memory to his heart' i.e. 'he does not cherish the king's memory'.
segments.s187-g143: بشورید is an imperative plural 'consult / take counsel' (b‑ prefix + شوریدن 'to deliberate' in classical usage), followed by با + a coordination of names 'with Gīv and Goudarz and the king' (با = with). The verb is plural imperative addressing multiple advisers.
segments.s187-g144: ز بهر is a poetic/prepositional phrase meaning 'for the sake of' (ز = poetic 'from/of', بهر = 'for/for the sake of'), followed by فریبرز (the personal name Fariburz) and the coordinated nouns تخت و کلاه 'throne and crown' (تخت = throne, کلاه = crown/hat, here 'crown').
segments.s187-g113: بپرسم is a 1st‑person present/subjunctive-form with the b‑prefix used in epic style to express the speaker's intention or deliberation ('I will/shall ask'). ز is the classical preposition 'of/from' introducing the person asked. که is a relative pronoun 'who/that' linking مردی to the embedded question; سالار is the head noun 'commander/chief' and کیست is the interrogative copula (کی 'who' + ست 'is') closing the embedded question.
segments.s187-g114: This short clause repeats an internal question about the famous person inside the host. اندرون 'within/inside' marks the location; نامبردار is an epithet meaning 'renowned' or 'noted'; کیست is again the interrogative copula 'who is'. The first item برزم is archaic/poetic and functions as a locative/scene‑setting word ('in the field/host') — its precise lexical sense is archaic here.
segments.s187-g115: یکی سور سازم is literal 'I will make one feast' where یکی functions as an indefinite numeral ('a certain / a') and سازم is 1st‑person present/future 'I build/make.' چنانچون توان is a concessive/comparative phrase 'as much as (my) ability allows' or 'to the extent I can,' with توان as 'ability/can'.
segments.s187-g116: ببینم is 1st‑person present/subjunctive ('may I see' / 'I will see') expressing intent. بشادی is an adverbial phrase 'with joy' placed before the object; رخ پهلوان is the noun phrase 'the hero's face' (رخ modified by پهلوان). The verb precedes its object here because of the optative/subordinate clause style common in poetic Persian.
segments.s187-g117: This is a series of partitives introduced by ز 'from' (classical/poetic) listing items: اسپ (steed/horse), شمشیر (sword), گرز (mace/club) and کمر (here 'girdle/belt'). The coordinating و 'and' links each item in the list; the repeated ز before شمشیر is a rhetorical/poetic repetition to emphasize each category.
segments.s187-g118: ببخشم is 1st‑person present/subjunctive or future‑oriented 'I will give/bestow.' The following z‑phrase ز هر چیز بسیار literally 'from every thing abundant' functions as the source or measure of largesse;مر is the 1st‑person enclitic ('to/for me'), so the full phrase indicates the speaker is bestowing or equipping himself with many items (i.e. 'I will give/furnish to myself many things').
segments.s187-g119: وزان پس is a temporal/discourse connector 'and from that / after that'; گرایم is 1st‑person present 'I turn' or 'I incline' and takes the prepositional complement به پیش سپاه 'toward the front of the army' (به 'to/toward' + پیش 'front/in the presence of' + سپاه 'army'). Together they mean 'then I turn toward the army.'
segments.s187-g120: بتوران combines the preposition بـ 'to/into' with توران (Turan, the enemy land) marking direction 'into Turan.' شوم is the 1st‑person subjunctive/optative 'may I become / I shall become.' داغ‌دل and کینه‌خواه are predicative epithets: داغ‌دل 'sorrow‑stricken / heart‑aches' and کینه‌خواه 'one who seeks revenge / avenger,' describing the state the speaker will assume.
segments.s187-g161: وگر introduces a conditional 'if' (classical/poetic). جز ز من = 'except from me / other than me' where ز is the archaic poetic variant of از (from). آید is the verbal kernel 'comes' and کسی at the end is the indefinite subject 'anyone' — the verb-before-subject order is a poetic inversion.
segments.s187-g162: نباید is an impersonal modal negative 'one should not / ought not'. بدو بودن is a verbal-nominal phrase 'to be with him' (بدو = to/with him, بودن = to be). ایمن بسی = 'quite/very safe' where بسی is an intensifier 'much/enough'; the whole clause is governed by the impersonal نباید, giving 'one ought not to be very safe with him'.
segments.s187-g163: نیاید is a negative optative/present form here meaning 'let not / should not come' (impersonal). بر تو is the prepositional target 'upon/against you'. به جز یک سوار is an exception phrase 'except a single horseman' (به + جز is the idiom for 'except'). The verb-first order is poetic; the sense is a prohibition that nobody should attack except one mounted man.
segments.s187-g164: چنینست is a copular/explanatory phrase 'such is / it is thus'. آیین این نامدار = 'the custom/practice of this famed one' with the demonstrative این + noun phrase following آیین; آیین here means 'custom/practice' rather than ritual.
segments.s187-g165: چو آید is a temporal clause 'when he comes' (چو = when). ببین is a 2nd-person imperative 'see / observe'. تا introduces the subordinate interrogative/clausal complement ('so that / to see') and چه آیدت رای = 'what counsel/plan comes to you' — آیدت has the clitic -ت 'to you' attached to the verb, and رای here means 'counsel/plan' (not merely 'opinion').
segments.s187-g166: در دژ ببند is a 2nd-person imperative phrase 'shut yourself in the fortress' (در دژ = in the citadel + ببند = close/lock). و مپرداز جای is a negative imperative 'and do not cast away/abandon the place' — مپرداز is the negative command form and جای means 'place/post', i.e. 'do not abandon your post.'
segments.s187-g167: یکی is an indefinite 'one / a certain' introducing a single actor or object. گرز پیروزه is a noun + adjective/descriptor 'turquoise (studded) mace' — پیروزه here means 'turquoise' (the stone/ornament). دسته بزر is a genitive/attributive phrase 'of the great troop/host' or 'of the large group' (دسته = group, بزر = great). The whole is a compressed poetic noun phrase describing the weapon.
segments.s187-g168: فرود آن زمان here functions as a temporal adverbial 'at that moment / then' (an archaic poetic use of فرود), followed by برکشید 'he lifted/drew up' (past tense) and از کمر 'from the waist/belt'. Thus the clause describes the action of drawing the mace from his belt at that moment.
segments.s187-g65: The line begins with فرود, here a subject/name (Farud), followed by a temporal adverbial آن زمان 'at that time', then the verb گفت 'said' introducing direct speech. The question that follows (سالار کیست) uses the interrogative کی + the copula ست (fused as کیست) = 'who is'. Word order is typical poetic Persian: Subject — adverbial — verb — quoted question.
segments.s187-g66: This is a continuation of the quoted question: برزم اندرون gives a locative/frame ('in/within the host/field') and نامبردار functions as a substantive adjective 'the famed/renowned one'. The interrogative clause ends again with کیست (کی + ست). The verb 'is' is supplied by ست; the full clause asks for identity of the famous man inside the ranks.
segments.s187-g67: بدو is a dative/prepositional pronoun 'to him' placed before the verb (so: 'to him said Bahram'), followed by گفت 'said' and the speaker's name بهرام. سالار طوس is an appositive/title: 'the commander, Tūs.' The line uses inverted/poetic order (indirect object first) common in epic verse.
segments.s187-g68: که introduces a relative clause modifying طوس ('who ...'). با اختر is a prepositional phrase 'with/under the star' (اختر used figuratively as an epithet), and کاویانست is an archaic/fused form (here read as a predicate attached to that relative clause). The clause closes with و کوس, adding 'and the drum' as another attribute; overall this is a descriptive relative clause 'who is with the star and (with) the war-drum.'
segments.s187-g69: ز گردان is a prepositional phrase 'from the troops/ranks'; چو functions as a comparative/partitive introducer 'such as' and lists proper names. The verb ('were' / 'came') is omitted — a common ellipsis in epic listing — so the names are presented as members of the host.
segments.s187-g70: Again چو begins a parallel list 'like' (continuing the enumeration). The names/epithets follow joined by و. نیو after فرهاد is an epithet 'ney (reed‑flute)', so 'Farhad the ney‑player' or 'Farhad with the ney.' The line is a stringing together of names/epithets without an explicit verb.
segments.s187-g71: This is another comparative list introduced by چو 'like'. گستهم and زنگه here appear as personal names (or epithets) in a catalogue of warriors; the coordinating و links items. Poetic diction omits verbs, so these names are listed as members of the force.
segments.s187-g72: This is a compact nominal phrase of names/titles: گرازه is likely a proper name, followed by سر 'head/chief' as an attributive element, مرد here 'man/hero' labels the person, and کنداوران is another name or epithet. The sequence names more warriors; the grammar is nominal and appositional, not a full clause with a finite verb.
segments.s187-g121: سزاوار here is a predicate meaning 'fitting/appropriate' followed by an infinitival complement جستن ('to seek'). این modifies that infinitive ('this seeking'), کین is the object of جستن ('this vengeance'), and منم (من + ام contracted) is the copular phrase 'I am' placed at the end in poetic inversion: literally 'fitting this to-seek vengeance I-am' → 'It is fitting that I seek this vengeance.'
segments.s187-g122: بجنگ functions adverbially/infinitival here ('in battle' / 'to fight'), followed by a noun phrase of epithets آتش تیز ('a keen/sharp fire') which describes the speaker's manner in combat; برزین is the personal name (Barzin) and منم again is the copula 'I am' placed at the end — poetic word order: 'In battle a sharp fire, Barzin I am.'
segments.s187-g123: سزدگر here introduces an evaluative/conditional idea 'if it be fitting' (an old/poetic particle), بگویی is the second‑person present/subjunctive/imperative-like form 'you speak / you should speak' (used with the conditional sense), تو is the explicit subject 'you', and با پهلوان marks the interlocutor 'with the champion'.
segments.s187-g124: که begins a relative clause 'who/that', آید is 'comes' (present/indicative or narrative), and the following compound برین سنگ روشن‌روان is a poetic noun phrase: بر+ین functions as a demonstrative/compound ('this/foremost' in this context), سنگ is 'stone/rock', and روشن‌روان is an epithet 'bright‑soul' modifying سنگ — together 'who comes to/at this bright‑souled rock.'
segments.s187-g125: بباشیم is a first‑person plural hortative/subjunctive 'let us be / let us remain'; یک هفته is a duration 'for one week'; ایدر (اینجا) is the locative 'here'; and بهم (بِهم) means 'together' — the whole phrase: 'let us remain here together for a week.'
segments.s187-g126: سگالیم is first‑person plural hortative 'let us assail/harry' (literally 'set dogs on' → 'attack/harry'), هرگونه is an adverbial 'in any manner / of any kind', and از بیش و کم forms an idiomatic range 'from more and less' = 'whether more or less' (از introduces the range 'from ... to ...'). Together: 'let us harry in every way, more or less.'
segments.s187-g127: به هشتم is a temporal phrase 'on the eighth (day)', چو is the temporal conjunction 'when', and برخیزد is a present/subjunctive verb 'rises / is raised'; آوای کوس is the subject 'the sound/cry of the drum' — 'When, on the eighth, the sound of the drum rises.'
segments.s187-g128: بزین is a verb 'to saddle up / mount' (here narrative/future sense 'will mount'), اندر آید is 'comes in/enters', and سپهدار طوس is the subject 'the commander Tous' (title + name) placed after the verb in poetic word order: 'the commander Tous will saddle up and come in.'
segments.s187-g97: کزان is a prepositional/relative-like particle here meaning “from that/of that,” introducing a description of the following noun. گونه in this line is the body‑part sense “cheek” (not “kind”), and بتگر is an agent noun “idol‑maker / image‑maker” placed next to چین; بپرگار is an adverbial phrase “with (a) compass” (بـ + پرگار) describing how the idol‑maker would draw, and چین is the head noun “fold / wrinkle.” Word order is poetic: the whole chunk sketches the specific kind of cheek and the little fold an artisan would try to mark out.
segments.s187-g98: نداند is a third‑person present/past‑style negative verb here meaning “does not (or did not) know.” نگارید functions as the substantive/infinitival idea “to portray / a depiction” (the thing not known how to be done). کس بر زمین is the collocation “no one on earth” (کس = anyone/person, بر زمین = on earth). The clause therefore says that no one on earth knows how to portray that fold/cheek.
segments.s187-g99: بدانست is a past tense verb “he/it knew” (knew it/recognized). کو here is an archaic relative/particle meaning roughly “who/that” (classical usage); grammatically it introduces the person being identified. از نژاد قباد is a prepositional phrase “from the race/lineage of Qobad,” completing the identification.
segments.s187-g100: ز (poetic variant of از) begins the prepositional phrase “from.” تخم سیاووش is a stock/metaphorical phrase “the seed (i.e. offspring/line) of Siyâvash.” دارد is the present‑tense verb “has / possesses” and نژاد is the noun “lineage / descent.” The clause therefore states possession of descent from Siyâvash; the word order is slightly inverted for poetic effect (noun phrase before دارد).
segments.s187-g101: برو here functions as an interjection/exclamation (classical usage similar to “bravo!”) rather than a literal imperative “go,” so it precedes آفرین as an exclamation. آفرین کرد is a past tense phrase “he expressed praise / uttered applause.” بردش is برد + the enclitic pronoun ـش (“took/led it/him”); with نماز as the following word it links the act of praise to the prayer (in context: he praised and the prayer was taken up / he led/raised the prayer). Note the poetic compression — بردش＋نماز is compact and can be read as “and he raised/led the prayer.”
segments.s187-g102: برآمد is a past verb “(it) arose / ascended.” ببالای is a prepositional/adverbial phrase “upward / on high” (ب + بالا). تند و دراز are two adjectives “swift and long” describing the manner of the rising (i.e. the prayer rose up quickly and stretched high). The sequence is typical poetic compression: verb + directional phrase + manner adjectives.
segments.s187-g103: فرود آمد is a compound verbal phrase meaning “he alighted / dismounted” (فرود = descent / alighting; آمد = came). از اسپ is the prepositional phrase “from the steed/horse,” and شاه جوان is the noun phrase “the young king” qualifying the horse (i.e. the young king’s steed). Word order is straightforward: verb + from + possessor phrase.
segments.s187-g104: نشست is the past tense “he sat / seated himself.” از بر is an archaic classical prepositional combination equivalent here to “upon / on,” so از بر سنگ means “upon the stone.” روشن‌روان is a compound epithet (literally “bright‑soul” or “clear‑faced”) modifying سنگ; together the phrase means he sat upon the ‘bright‑soul stone.’
segments.s187-g129: میان را ببندم بکین پدر: میان را (میان + را) is the direct object 'the middle / the gap' (را marks it definite/object). ببندم is 1st-person singular present-subjunctive used here with a future/intent sense: 'I will close / shut.' بِکین is an archaic/poetic form that can mean 'vengeance' or an imperative 'do' — here it is best read as the noun 'vengeance' ('with vengeance'); پدر is a vocative 'father' addressing someone. The whole segment ties as 'I will close the gap, with vengeance, father.'
segments.s187-g130: یکی جنگ سازم بدرد جگر: یکی 'one / a single' sets number. جنگ سازم is 'I will make / set up a battle' (سازم = present-subjunctive/intent 'I will make'). بدرد is here causative/resulting 'that will cause pain / rend' (not 'be useful'), and جگر is used metaphorically as 'heart / seat of feeling.' Together: 'I will contrive a single battle that will pierce/rend the heart.'
segments.s187-g131: که با شیر جنگ آشنایی دهد: که introduces a subordinate/clause of purpose or result ('so that' / 'that'). با + شیر = 'with the lion' (instrumental/association). جنگ آشنایی دهد literally 'gives/teaches familiarity with war' — دهد is 3rd-person singular present/subjunctive/simple present 'gives' or 'will give/teach.' The clause expresses the intended result: 'that he may make the lion acquainted with war.'
segments.s187-g132: ز نر پر کرگس گوایی دهد: ز (poetic ز) = 'from / by'; نر = 'the male' (here likely 'the male of a species'); پر کرگس = 'feather of the vulture' (پر = 'feather', کرگس = 'vulture'), and گوایی here is adverbial 'seemingly / so that he appears' (گوایی دهد = 'gives the appearance/semblance'). دهد again is 'gives' (3rd-sing.). The whole segment says roughly 'and make it give the semblance / appearance (from the male's vulture-feather),' i.e. produce a certain appearance/effect connected to the vulture's feather.
segments.s187-g133: که اندر جهان کینه را زین نشان: که again introduces result ('that/so that'). اندر جهان = 'in the world.' کینه را = 'hatred' as a definite object (را marks it). زین نشان = 'this sign/token of the saddle' (زین = 'saddle' or trappings, نشان = 'sign / token'). The phrase sets up what should not occur in the world: 'that hatred (as marked) by this saddle-token...' (prepares the idea continued in the next segment).
segments.s187-g134: نبندد میان کس ز گردنکشان: نبندد is 3rd-person singular present-subjunctive/optative with negative sense 'may not bind / will not bind.' میان کس 'between anyone' (میان = 'between / among', کس = 'any person'); ز گردنکشان = 'from/of the neck-pullers' i.e. 'the rebels / proud ones.' The clause thus completes the thought: '...no one among the rebels shall bind (close up) hatred by this sign.'
segments.s187-g135: بدو گفت بهرام کای شهریار: بدو = 'to him' (prepositional/indirect object). گفت here functions as the verb 'said' (classical diction uses گفت for 'said'); بهرام is the speaker's name. کای is a vocative 'O' (archaic/Kayanian title), شهریار = 'king' (vocative/title). Word order is 'to him said Bahram: O king...' — a typical classical Persian order where the indirect object appears before گفت.
segments.s187-g136: جوان و هنرمند و گرد و سوار: a string of epithets linked by و 'and'. جوان = 'young.' هنرمند = 'skillful / accomplished' (here an epithet of the king). گرد in epic diction often appears as a short epithet paired with سوار; here it functions as part of a compound description and should be read as an archaic/epic adjective related to martial prowess, and سوار = 'rider / horseman.' The sequence lists qualities of the addressee: 'young and skillful and (valiant) and a horseman.'
